ab: na mun’ica asu?
ch: A:你要去哪裡?
en: A: Where are you going?

ab: munha’an cak pisanglavan macuaz sanglav.
ch: B:我要去菜園裡種菜。
en: B: I’m going to plant vegetables in the garden.

ab: malkama’aq qa asu?
ch: A:你在做什麼?
en: A: What are you doing?

ab: muav madia’ a ismut, unzaku maqun, na culan zaku danum unisin.
ch: B:草太多了，我在砍草，等一下要澆水。
en: B:There’s too much grass, I’m cutting it, I’ll have to water it later.

ab: kadangazin cak siza’ i kaul cin tanga’ u?
ch: A:幫我把鐮刀和鋤頭拿來好嗎?
en: A: Will you get my scythes and hoes, please?

ab: pica’un qa su’u?
ch: B:你放在哪裡呢?
en: B: Where did you put it?

ab: iha’an ngalan ni tanga’-tikutuh.
ch: A:放在犁耙旁邊。
en: A: Put it next to the plow rake.

ab: leqangvangin asu?
ch: A:你翻過土了嗎?
en: A: Did you turn over the soil?

ab: laupang leqangvanan, na pinangan mac lakiun.
ch: B:我剛翻過土，等等要播種。
en: B: I just turned over the soil, and so on to sow seeds.

ab: maqtu’in na sincuaz su sanglav kaunun?
ch: A:你種的菜可以收成了嗎?
en: A: Are your vegetables ready to be harvested?

ab: maqtu’in na kanlang iti siza’un, ni’ang ita sanglav-samaq maqtu siza’un.
ch: B:這裡的高麗菜可以收成了，那裡的昭和草就還不能收成。
en: B: The cabbage here is ready to be harvested, but the Showa grass there is not yet ready to be harvested.

ab: ai! masu’ madia a hutan sinkai.
ch: A:哇!你挖了這麼多的地瓜。
en: A:  Wow! You dug up so many gourds.

ab: kadangazin piha’an suku’ u?
ch: B:幫我放穀倉好嗎?
en: B: Will you help me with the barn?

ab: malkama’aq qa amu?
ch: A:你們在做什麼?
en: A: What are you doing?

ab: padangi cam mi ami’ madamu ki iskan.
ch: B:我們在放漁網抓魚。
en: B: We’re releasing fishing nets to catch fish.

ab: madia iti a iskan?
ch: A:這裡的魚多嗎?
en: A: Are there many fish here?

ab: madia’in na iskan dinamu ha’an kalala’.
ch: B:魚簍裡已經抓了很多魚。
en: B: The fish basket has been full of fish.

ab: kuma’aq asu madamu iskan?
ch: A:你平常怎麼抓魚?
en: A: How do you usually catch fish?

ab: kucia cak ami’ madamu ki iskan.
ch: B:我都用魚網子補魚。
en: B: I use fish nets to replenish fish.

ab: makucia asu buculkahkah qaqanup?
ch: A:你會用弓箭打獵嗎?
en: A: Do you know how to hunt with a bow and arrow?

ab: maqanglac cak malhaqu, mintun madas cullku’ cin acu’ munba’av qanup.
ch: B:我通常都是用陷阱抓獵物，偶爾也會拿槍帶狗狩獵。
en: B: I usually catch my prey with a trap, but occasionally I hunt with a gun and a dog.

ab: laqaqaiban ni vanis sa dan di.
ch: A:這是山豬所走的路。
en: A: This is the road the boar took.

ab: masu maqanciap?
ch: B:你怎麼知道呢?
en: B: How do you know?

ab: cia qa di vanis dapan, anangqa laqaqaiban aipi’ vanis.
ch: A:這是山豬的腳印，所以這是山豬所走的路。
en: A: These are the footprints of the boar, so this is the path the boar took.

ab: ina’ hiqiv ata haqu’, maupat haiza a tishaqu’.
ch: A:快去看我們的陷阱，好像抓到獵物了。
en: A: Go and see our trap, for it seems that we have caught our prey.

ab: masu maqanciap?
ch: B:你怎麼知道呢?
en: B: How do you know?

ab: maupat sak ka acu’, i’na’ hiav ata kantulun.
ch: A:狗好像聞到味道了。我們快跟過去。
en: A: The dog seems to have smelled it. Let’s follow them.

ab: ma’aq qa tas’iun su’u ki’?
ch: A:你正在製作的是什麼東西?
en: A: What are you working on?

ab: malaqatu, na madamu ki qamutic.
ch: B:這是石壓式陷阱，用來抓老鼠的。
en: B: This is a stone trap, used to catch rats.

ab: ma’aq qa pi’ita’un su’u?
ch: A:你放在那裡的是什麼東西?
en: A: What is that thing you put there?

ab: ma’aipa’ a haqumucqut, malhaqu’ ki cakut.
ch: B:那是套索式繩索陷阱，我們用它來抓山羌。
en: B: It was a lasso rope trap, and we used it to catch Shankiang.

ab: matumasqing tamadiqanin, madia mita haip pa i’nama’.
ch: A:感謝祖靈賜福，今天我們有很多獵物收獲。
en: A: Thanks to the blessing of our ancestors, we had a great harvest today.

ab: tuza’! dasun ita munacang kaskun paqusil!
ch: B:是啊!我們帶回部落好好分享吧!
en: B: Yeah! Let’s take it back to the tribe and share it!
